epigraph.text: Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their toil.
epigraph.quote: Comparison is the basis of all scientific inference.

content.text: Often we compare two groups rather than one group to a fixed value.

content.title: Two-Sample $t$-Test
content.content:
To test $H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2$:
\[
 t = \frac{(\xbar_1 - \xbar_2) - 0}{\sqrt{S_1^2/n_1 + S_2^2/n_2}}
\]
with approximate degrees of freedom from Welch's formula.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Comparing Two Means
content.problem:
Group 1: $n_1 = 30$, $\xbar_1 = 84$, $S_1 = 12$.
Group 2: $n_2 = 35$, $\xbar_2 = 78$, $S_2 = 10$.

Test if means differ at $\alpha = 0.05$.

content.text: When observations are naturally paired (before/after, matched subjects):

content.title: Paired $t$-Test
content.content:
For paired differences $d_i = X_{1i} - X_{2i}$, test $H_0: \mu_d = 0$:
\[
 t = \frac{\bar{d} - 0}{S_d / \sqrt{n}}
\]
with $df = n - 1$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Two-Sample $z$-Test for Proportions
content.content:
To test $H_0: p_1 = p_2$:
\[
 z = \frac{\phat_1 - \phat_2}{\sqrt{\phat(1-\phat)(1/n_1 + 1/n_2)}}
\]
where $\phat = \frac{X_1 + X_2}{n_1 + n_2}$ is the pooled proportion.
content.label: Theorem
